title: Retrieve-then-rerank: a coarse field proposes a shortlist, a pairwise comparator decides among it
hypothesis: Two independent diagnostics from project memory (champion-failure-mode-probes) show the true cell is the champion's field top-1 pick only ~2% of the time despite landing in its own top-20 (of 1024) on 33-58% of crops in every lighting bucket: the descriptor already narrows the answer down, but a single 620-dim-input linear scorer (one fixed hyperplane per cell, trained without ever knowing which OTHER cells are in contention for a given crop) cannot finish separating a handful of genuine near-ties from the truth. Nine straight rounds (36-44) attacked this from every angle EXCEPT the one where a model gets a second, focused look at just its own top candidates: two margin-loss attempts (34, 36) pushed the SAME one-shot linear scores further apart without adding any comparison capacity; four architecture rewrites (39 coordinate-generated field, 40 coarse-to-fine field+offset, 31 neural-map correlation) reparameterized the SAME one-shot decision; four rounds (41/42/43/44) replaced the trunk's training objective (contrastive, reconstruction-joint, reconstruction-sequential) hoping a better descriptor would let the SAME one-shot linear head finish the job on its own. None of them gave the model machinery to explicitly ask 'given that it's probably cell A, B, or C, which one actually matches this crop's fine layout?' -- every design has forced one linear pass over 1024 independent hyperplanes to answer both 'roughly where' and 'precisely which of the confusable lookalikes' at once. This experiment splits that into two mechanically different stages: a coarse field (architecturally similar to the proven proposer, kept for cheap, well-calibrated shortlisting) picks the top-8 candidate cells, then a SEPARATE pairwise comparator -- a small MLP that explicitly takes the DIFFERENCE and PRODUCT between the crop's own fine local descriptor and each candidate cell's learned identity embedding -- scores only those 8, with capacity to spend specifically on telling near-ties apart rather than diluting a fixed linear budget across 1024 simultaneous binary decisions it never actually has to make at once. Before committing to this, I checked the last two rounds' raw train logs for a scientific-integrity issue: runs/20260722_214356_iter3/train_berlin.log and train_prignitz.log (exp 43) AND runs/20260722_231953_iter4/train_berlin.log and train_munich.log (exp 44) are all 0 bytes -- crashed before a single epoch printed -- while the other two areas in each run trained cleanly to a healthy converged loss every time. Two different area-subsets failed across the two runs (not the same pair), which rules out anything area-specific and points at loop.sh's four-way concurrent GPU launch (autoresearch/loop.sh:337-347) racing on CUDA context/memory init; the one thing exp 43 and exp 44 shared that earlier from-scratch rounds (41, 42) did not was a full encoder-DECODER shape reconstructing a 128x128x3 image (large intermediate activations at high spatial resolution), which is a plausible reason those two specific rounds tipped some subset of the 4 concurrent processes over a memory/timing edge that plain encoder-only designs (41, 42) never hit. Both were therefore NEVER actually refuted at the design level -- gated 1e9 by an infra race, not by their science -- and library L1 (both flavors: flying pre-model, training-only reconstruction auxiliary) is the common thread behind both crashes, so this design deliberately avoids any full-resolution pixel-reconstruction path and stays encoder-only (a small MLP over pooled descriptors, no decoder, no upsampling), both because I have no revised hypothesis that reconstruction itself was the problem and because I want this round's result to be readable as a real test of retrieve-then-rerank, not a third coin-flip against the same launch race.
method: In model/model.py: delete the ImageNet-pretrained MobileNetV3-Small trunk entirely (no torchvision import, no checkpoint load) and replace it with a from-scratch 4-block conv stem (3->24->48->80->112 channels, stride 2 each block) that is architecturally distinct from the plain 4-stage stack reused identically across exps 41/42/43/44 -- it exposes TWO taps: the final 8x8x112 map (for the coarse stage, as before) and the intermediate 16x16x80 map (new, for the fine reranker). Coarse stage: GAP(8x8x112) + a 1x1-conv layout squeeze, into a Linear(620,1024) field head, trained with the SAME Gaussian-smoothed CE as the current champion -- this stage's only job is to propose a top-8 shortlist, not to be the final answer, so it is deliberately left close to the proven mechanism. NEW candidate re-rank stage: a 1x1 conv squeezes the 16x16x80 detail tap to 16 channels, GAP'd to a 16-d 'detail descriptor' per crop; a from-scratch nn.Embedding(1024, 16) gives every map cell a learned identity vector; for each of the coarse stage's top-8 candidate cells, a shared 2-layer MLP (64->32->1) scores [detail_desc, cell_embedding, |detail_desc-cell_embedding|, detail_desc*cell_embedding] -- a small pairwise comparator, evaluated 8 times per crop, that can spend nonlinear capacity specifically on the confusable-pair discrimination the diagnosed wall names, something a single 1024-way linear layer structurally cannot do. Decode and confidence move from the 1024-way field to this K=8 rerank output (sharpened soft-argmax over just the 8 candidate cell centers; confidence from the top1-vs-top2 margin of the K-way softmax). C4 rotation voting (kept, changed only in scope): all 4 rotations still feed the coarse stage exactly as today (geometric-mean logit average, unchanged mechanism) so shortlist quality keeps the proven heading-robustness; the fine reranker reads only the as-given (unrotated) crop's detail tap, both to bound compute/memory (see crash forensics above) and because its job -- comparing THIS crop's actual texture against a handful of candidate cells -- does not need the same rotation-marginalization the coarse proposer does. Lighting gate: removed (no day/night expert blend), consistent with every from-scratch round since 41 -- the two-stage split is the new mechanism for handling appearance ambiguity, not a lighting-conditional branch. Loss (loss_fn): coarse Gaussian-CE (unchanged formula) + a new rerank cross-entropy (target = the true cell's position within the K candidate indices, with the true cell force-included in the training-time candidate set by replacing the coarse stage's weakest of the top-8 whenever it isn't already present, so the reranker's gradient always reaches a real comparison against the truth) + the existing coordinate L2 (now against the K-way decode) + the existing confidence BCE, unchanged weights. model/train.py: remove the pretrained-vs-head 10x LR split (single Adam group at 1e-3, since nothing is pretrained anymore, matching exp 44's precedent) and add one line computing each batch's true cell index from yb to pass into forward() for the forced-inclusion rule; sample_epoch, compute_cell_weights (confusability sampler, exp 35, kept), prepare_realizations, calibrate_conf_shift, and the epoch/schedule constants are all untouched verbatim.
expected_outcome: If the ranking wall is a one-shot-linear-scorer capacity problem rather than a descriptor-content problem (the thing all four prior descriptor-objective attempts jointly failed to fix), worst-case median error improves from the champion's 743.07 m to roughly 450-650 m (12-39%), because the reranker only has to solve an 8-way problem drawn from a shortlist the coarse stage already gets right 33-58% of the time -- a far more tractable target than the diagnosed 2%-top-1-of-1024. Mechanism-level signature to check post-hoc: shortlist inclusion rate (true cell within the coarse stage's own top-8, measured train-time, cheap to log) should land in a similar 15-40% ballpark to the historical top-20-of-1024 rate scaled down; if the reranker is doing real work, final top-1 accuracy among crops where the true cell IS in the shortlist should be dramatically higher than the champion's blanket 2%. Falsification forks: (a) if shortlist inclusion rate is low (well under 15%), the coarse stage itself -- not final ranking -- is the bottleneck, and the reranker never gets a fair shot; the next iteration should widen K or spend more capacity on the proposer, not the comparator; (b) if shortlist inclusion is healthy but the primary metric doesn't improve over 743.07 m, the pairwise-comparator hypothesis is refuted at the mechanism level -- a shared 64-d MLP over difference/product features isn't enough discriminative capacity either, and the next iteration should try a deeper/wider comparator or an explicit spatial (not just pooled) comparison rather than another shortlist-generation scheme; (c) if the metric regresses sharply despite both of the above looking healthy, the two-stage split likely introduced a train/inference mismatch (e.g. the forced-inclusion rule during training makes the reranker's training distribution of candidate sets easier than what it sees at eval, when the true cell is sometimes genuinely absent from the top-8) -- the fix would be to train on the RAW top-8 (with an explicit 'not present' outcome) at least part of the time rather than always forcing inclusion.
eli5: Picture the old system as one judge who has to instantly rank 1,024 possible neighborhoods for every photo, alone, in one shot -- it's good at narrowing things down to a handful of look-alikes but bad at picking the right one among them. This experiment splits that into two judges: a fast first judge still narrows the whole map down to its best 8 guesses (same job as before), but now a second, more careful judge is brought in ONLY to compare those 8 finalists side-by-side against the photo's fine details, the way you'd hold a few candidate street corners up next to a snapshot and look for what actually matches, rather than trying to rank all 1,024 at a glance. We also swap out the borrowed off-the-shelf vision backbone for a new one built from scratch, since the old one is off-limits this round.

stages.name: Camera frame
stages.detail: 128×128 px aerial crop, one of 6 simulated lighting conditions

stages.name: Rotation fan
stages.detail: the crop and its three 90° pixel rotations all feed the feature extractor and coarse field for the heading-robust shortlist vote, same C4 mechanism as before; the NEW fine comparator stage, unlike the coarse stage, only ever looks at the as-given (unrotated) crop's own detail features -- it isn't asked to be heading-invariant, only to compare this exact crop against a handful of named candidates

stages.name: Feature extractor
stages.detail: a new from-scratch, 4-block conv stack (3->24->48->80->112 channels, stride 2 each block, no borrowed ImageNet weights, no MobileNetV3 family, no checkpoint file) -- structurally different from the plain stack reused in the last four rounds because it keeps and exposes an intermediate finer-resolution tap (16x16x80) alongside the final coarse tap (8x8x112), feeding two separate downstream jobs instead of one

stages.name: Layout summary
stages.detail: same recipe as before -- a 1x1 conv squeezes the final 8x8 feature grid into a spatial code, kept alongside the pooled texture average -- resized for the new 112-channel trunk; feeds only the coarse proposer stage

stages.name: Lighting gate
stages.detail: REMOVED: no day/night expert blend. The new two-stage retrieve-then-rerank split is this round's mechanism for handling appearance ambiguity instead of a lighting-conditional branch

stages.name: Probability map
stages.detail: a coarse field (same Gaussian-smoothed-CE-trained linear scorer shape as the old single field, now explicitly a PROPOSER, not the final answer) reads the layout summary into a heat map over all 1024 map cells and its own top 8 cells become the shortlist for the next stage

stages.name: Candidate shortlist
stages.detail: NEW stage: the coarse map's top-8 highest-scoring cells (plus, during training only, the true cell forced in if the coarse stage missed it) become the small candidate set the rest of the pipeline actually decides between

stages.name: Candidate re-rank
stages.detail: NEW stage: a 16-d 'what does this crop's fine detail look like' descriptor (pooled from the trunk's finer 16x16 tap) is compared against each shortlisted cell's own learned 16-d identity vector by a small shared comparator network (looking at their difference and product, not just a plain dot product), producing one refined score per candidate -- capacity spent specifically on telling a handful of look-alikes apart, instead of one linear pass trying to rank all 1024 at once

stages.name: Decode
stages.detail: contrast-sharpened balance-point (soft-argmax) over just the 8 re-ranked candidates' positions, instead of over the full 1024-cell field -- the same sharpening mechanism as before, now applied to a much smaller, more confident set of options

stages.name: Confidence
stages.detail: reads how much the re-rank stage's top pick beats its runner-up (plus the re-rank field's own spread) instead of the old 1024-way heat map's shape -- same general idea (a clean win vs. a toss-up), computed on the new, sharper 8-way comparison

stages.name: Training signal
stages.detail: the coarse proposer keeps training on the usual 'light up the true cell' grading (Gaussian-smoothed cross-entropy over all 1024 cells) so it stays a reliable shortlister; NEW: the re-rank stage separately trains on 'pick the true cell out of your 8 finalists' (cross-entropy over just the shortlist, with the true cell always included as an option during training so the comparator always gets to learn from a real answer); the existing coordinate-accuracy and confidence-calibration grading carry over unchanged, just computed on the new 8-way decode

stages.name: Training schedule
stages.detail: since nothing is borrowed/pretrained anymore, all parameters (feature extractor, both heads, the comparator, the cell identity vectors) train together in one learning-rate group instead of the old slow-trunk/fast-head split reserved for gently fine-tuning pretrained features; same total epoch budget and cosine schedule shape as before

implementation_brief:
Frozen contracts to preserve exactly, restated: train.py's CLI stays `python -m model.train --area <area> --out-dir <dir> [--epochs N] [--data-dir DIR] [--max-crops-per-bucket N] [--seed N]` with the same train_info.json append-and-print behavior. model.py's export_onnx()/ONNX contract stays exactly: torch.onnx.export(model, torch.zeros(1,3,128,128), path, input_names=['frame'], output_names=['uvc'], opset_version=17, dynamo=False), and model.forward(x) called with ONLY the positional input tensor (the exact call export_onnx and estimate_position/calibrate_conf_shift make) must return a single (N,3) float tensor [[u,v,conf]] with u,v in normalized map coords and conf in [0,1] -- do not change this default-call return shape. Deployment gates: exported ONNX must stay <= 4 MiB per area (this design's total param count is far smaller than the old mobilenet-based one -- no need for any quantization trick this round) and the host latency proxy <= 250 ms.

model/model.py: (1) Delete `from torchvision.models import mobilenet_v3_small`, `_build_pretrained_trunk`, `PRETRAINED_TRUNK_PATH`, `IMAGENET_MEAN`, `IMAGENET_STD`, and the `norm_mean`/`norm_std` buffers -- no ImageNet dependency anywhere, no reference to the old checkpoint filename or class name in code OR comments/docstrings. (2) Add `RERANK_K = 8` and `DETAIL_CH = 16` module constants alongside the existing GRID_K/TARGET_SIGMA_CELLS/DECODE_BETA/GOOD_ERR_UV (all four of those existing constants and their values are unchanged). (3) New `FeatureTrunk(nn.Module)`: four `nn.Sequential(nn.Conv2d(...,3,stride=2,padding=1), nn.BatchNorm2d(...), nn.ReLU(inplace=True))` blocks, channels 3->24->48->80->112; forward() runs them in sequence and returns BOTH the block-2 output (16x16x80, name it `detail_fmap`) and the block-3/final output (8x8x112, name it `fmap`) as a tuple -- do not collapse this to one tensor. (4) In `TinyLocNet.__init__`: replace `self.features = _build_pretrained_trunk()` with `self.trunk = FeatureTrunk()`; probe both output shapes with a zeros(1,3,128,128) forward at init time (as the existing code already does) to size the downstream layers generically (no hardcoded 8/16/24 spatial dims baked in beyond what the probe derives, consistent with the project's no-area-specific-constants rule -- these are architecture-shape constants derived from a fixed 128x128 input, which is fine, not an area-specific constant). Keep `self.layout_squeeze` (Conv2d(fmap_ch, 8, 1)) and add `self.coarse_logits = nn.Linear(fmap_ch + 8*fmap_hw*fmap_hw, GRID_K*GRID_K)` (replaces the old gated dual-expert `loc_logits`/`dark_logits`/`gate` -- delete all three, and delete `self.dark_logits`, `self.gate`). Add `self.detail_squeeze = nn.Conv2d(detail_ch, DETAIL_CH, 1)` (1x1 conv on the 16x16 tap), `self.cell_embed = nn.Embedding(GRID_K*GRID_K, DETAIL_CH)`, `self.rerank_mlp = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(DETAIL_CH*4, 32), nn.ReLU(), nn.Linear(32, 1))`. Update `self.conf_head` to take the new input width (DETAIL_CH + 3: margin, entropy, gap) instead of the old (feat_ch+3). Keep `self.conf_shift` buffer and `self.cell_u`/`self.cell_v` buffers (GRID_K-derived, unchanged `_grid_centers` helper). (5) Add a module-level helper `def cell_index_from_uv(u, v, k=GRID_K): return (v.clamp(0, 0.999999) * k).long().clamp(0, k-1) * k + (u.clamp(0, 0.999999) * k).long().clamp(0, k-1)` (mirrors the existing inline formula train.py's `compute_cell_weights`/train_area already use for the identical purpose -- keep it in exactly one place now, model.py, and have train.py import it instead of re-deriving it if train.py has its own copy; check train.py's `crop_cell` computation in `train_area` and leave that one alone, it indexes a NumPy array of static crop centers for the confusability sampler, a different use than the new per-batch torch tensor helper). (6) Rewrite `forward(self, x, target_cell_idx=None, return_aux=False)`: n=x.shape[0]; x4=_c4_stack(x) (unchanged helper); run `detail4, fmap4 = self.trunk(x4)`; `f4 = fmap4.mean(dim=(2,3))`; `layout4 = self.layout_squeeze(fmap4).flatten(1)`; `coarse_logits4 = self.coarse_logits(torch.cat([f4, layout4], dim=1))`; `coarse_logits = coarse_logits4.reshape(4, n, -1).mean(dim=0)` (unchanged C4-average pattern); `detail_fmap = detail4[:n]` (first n rows of the 4n stack are the UNROTATED crop -- `_c4_stack` concatenates `[x, r90, r180, r270]` in that order, so index [0:n] is the as-given orientation; state this indexing explicitly in a code comment since getting it wrong silently breaks the 'reranker sees the real crop' contract); `detail_desc = self.detail_squeeze(detail_fmap).mean(dim=(2,3))` ([n, DETAIL_CH]); `candidate_idx = coarse_logits.topk(RERANK_K, dim=1).indices` ([n,K], sorted descending -- torch topk default); if `target_cell_idx is not None`: `in_shortlist = (candidate_idx == target_cell_idx.unsqueeze(1)).any(dim=1)`; `candidate_idx = candidate_idx.clone()`; `candidate_idx[~in_shortlist, -1] = target_cell_idx[~in_shortlist]` (replace the weakest -- last, since topk is sorted descending -- candidate with the true cell for rows that missed it); `cell_emb = self.cell_embed(candidate_idx)` ([n,K,DETAIL_CH]); `detail_exp = detail_desc.unsqueeze(1).expand(-1, RERANK_K, -1)`; `feats = torch.cat([detail_exp, cell_emb, (detail_exp-cell_emb).abs(), detail_exp*cell_emb], dim=-1)`; `rerank_logits = self.rerank_mlp(feats).squeeze(-1)` ([n,K]); `p = torch.softmax(DECODE_BETA*rerank_logits, dim=1)`; gather `cand_u = self.cell_u[candidate_idx]`, `cand_v = self.cell_v[candidate_idx]` (index the existing static buffers by the candidate indices); `u=(p*cand_u).sum(1,keepdim=True)`, `v=(p*cand_v).sum(1,keepdim=True)`; `p1=torch.softmax(rerank_logits,dim=1)`; `u1,v1` same pattern unsharpened; `pd=p.detach()`; `top2 = pd.topk(min(2,RERANK_K), dim=1).values`; `margin = top2[:,0:1] - (top2[:,1:2] if top2.shape[1]>1 else torch.zeros_like(top2[:,0:1]))`; `ent = -(pd*torch.log(pd+1e-9)).sum(1,keepdim=True)/float(np.log(RERANK_K))`; `gap = torch.sqrt((u.detach()-u1.detach())**2+(v.detach()-v1.detach())**2+1e-12)`; `z=self.conf_head(torch.cat([detail_desc.detach(), margin, ent, gap], dim=1))`; `conf=torch.sigmoid(z-self.conf_shift)`; `out=torch.cat([u,v,conf],dim=1)`; `if return_aux: return out, coarse_logits, rerank_logits, candidate_idx`; `return out`. (7) Rewrite `loss_fn(pred, coarse_logits, rerank_logits, candidate_idx, target_uv)`: keep the existing Gaussian-smoothed CE block verbatim but rename its output `coarse_ce` (computed against `coarse_logits`, same GRID_K/TARGET_SIGMA_CELLS formula, unchanged); add `target_idx = cell_index_from_uv(target_uv[:,0], target_uv[:,1])`; `match = (candidate_idx == target_idx.unsqueeze(1))`; `rerank_target_pos = match.float().argmax(dim=1)` (safe: forward()'s forced-inclusion guarantees exactly one match whenever target_cell_idx was passed, which it always will be from train.py); `rerank_ce = nn.functional.cross_entropy(rerank_logits, rerank_target_pos)`; keep `coord_loss` and `conf_loss` blocks verbatim (they only read `pred`/`target_uv`, unaffected by the rest); `return coarse_ce + rerank_ce + coord_loss + 0.3*conf_loss`. (8) `export_onnx` and `estimate_position`: unchanged (they only call `model(dummy)`/`sess.run` with the single-tensor contract, already preserved by forward()'s default args).

model/train.py: (1) In `train_area`, replace the `trunk_params`/`head_params`/two-LR-group `opt = torch.optim.Adam([...])` block with a single group: `opt = torch.optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=1e-3)` (delete the `trunk_params`/`trunk_ids`/`head_params` lines entirely). (2) In the batch loop, before computing loss: `target_idx = model_module.cell_index_from_uv(yb[:,0], yb[:,1])` (import `cell_index_from_uv` alongside the existing `GRID_K, build_model, export_onnx, loss_fn` import from `model.model`), then `out, coarse_logits, rerank_logits, candidate_idx = model(xb, target_cell_idx=target_idx, return_aux=True)` (replaces the old `out, logits = model(xb, return_logits=True)`), then `loss = loss_fn(out, coarse_logits, rerank_logits, candidate_idx, yb)`. (3) Update the `info["init"]` string to describe the new from-scratch retrieve-then-rerank design (no mention of mobilenet/pretrained anywhere). (4) Everything else in train.py -- `compute_cell_weights`, `prepare_realizations`, `sample_epoch`, `calibrate_conf_shift` (which calls `model(xb)` with defaults, still valid), the CLI/argparse block, `CAL_CROPS_PER_BUCKET`/`MIN_KEEP_RATE`/`TRAIN_REALIZATIONS`/`EPOCH_MULT`/`CONFUSABILITY_ALPHA`/`NEIGHBOR_EXCLUDE_R` constants -- stays byte-for-byte unchanged.
